name: Baked Stuffed Red Snapper
recipeIngredient: 5 Lb. whole red snapper, cleaned and dressed (5 to 6)
recipeInstructions:
Rinse fish thoroughly in cold running water to remove all blood and viscera. Pat dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, combine 1/4 teaspoon salt (if desired), 1/8 teaspoon pepper, celery seed, thyme, garlic, and juice; mix well. Rub fish well with mixture, inside and out.
Cover and refrigerate in a large rectangular baking dish for 2 hours.
To make stuffing, melt 3 tablespoons butter over low heat in a medium saucepan. When hot, add onions, bell pepper, and garlic. Saute vegetables several minutes until tender. Remove saucepan from heat; stir in bread crumbs, olives, peanuts, remaining salt, and pepper.
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Remove fish from refrigerator. Fill with stuffing mixture. Dot fish with remaining butter. Bake for 40 minutes or until fish flakes when tested with a fork. Remove from oven; transfer to serving platter. Garnish with watercress and lime wedges. Makes 6 servings.
